hero.question: Will Israel strike 3 countries in 2026?
hero.prob_take: At 13.8%, the crowd is pricing this as a tail risk event that requires a major escalation in regional conflict, essentially betting against a third Israeli military operation by year-end. The real question is whether 2026 looks materially different from 2025 (hint: the market's baseline assumes current tensions don't metastasize), which makes this a cheap hedge on Lebanon or a broader regional blowup, but the price doesn't properly discount how quickly Middle East dynamics shift.

daily_take.headline: Israel's 3-Country Strike Odds Jump 6.5 Points. Smell the Iran Escalation Trade.
daily_take.deck: The market repriced Israel's odds of striking 3 countries in 2026 from 7.3% to 13.8% in 24 hours on $1.9M volume. This tracks renewed Iran tensions and Trump's hawkish posture on Middle East policy. The real tell: this price assumes only modest regional escalation, but historical precedent says once Israel moves, it moves decisively. Shorts look exposed.
